From inspiration cards and AI infographics to subtitles, translation, and lip-sync dubbing — we bridge inspiration and production, helping creators publish faster.

controlled.title: Controlled Video Generation

metadata.title: Free Bilingual Subtitles Generator | Curify AI
metadata.description: Create bilingual subtitles for your videos with Curify’s free AI-powered tool. Perfect for YouTube, TikTok, education, and global creators.

bilingual.title: Bilingual Subtitles Made Easy
bilingual.description: Automatically generate subtitles in two languages using Curify’s AI subtitle engine. Ideal for creators, educators, and global businesses.
bilingual.example: Example: Jensen Huang explaining AI strategy — English + Chinese subtitles auto-generated.

why.title: Why Choose Curify for Bilingual Subtitles?
why.point1: Supports over 170 languages with accurate translation.
why.point3: Perfect for YouTube, TikTok, and educational videos.
why.point4: Export ready-to-publish subtitle files or embedded videos.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Yes, Curify offers a free plan with limited subtitle minutes per month.
faq.q2: Can I edit subtitles after generation?
faq.a2: Absolutely. You can adjust timing, translation, and style before exporting.

what.title: What Are Bilingual Subtitles?
what.p1: Bilingual subtitles display two languages simultaneously on screen — typically the original spoken language and a translated version directly below it. Instead of replacing the original captions, bilingual subtitles preserve the source language while adding a synchronized translation.
what.p2: This format is widely used in language learning videos, international YouTube channels, online courses, conference recordings, and global marketing. It helps viewers compare meaning in real time, improves comprehension, and expands accessibility without re-recording your video.
what.p3: For example, a tech keynote delivered in English can show English on the first line and Chinese, Spanish, or Japanese on the second line — turning one video into a global asset.

how.title: How Curify Generates High-Quality Bilingual Subtitles
how.p1: Curify is not a simple caption generator. Our bilingual subtitle engine is built on a multi-stage AI translation pipeline designed for accuracy, timing precision, and contextual understanding.
how.p2: First, we transcribe your video using high-accuracy speech recognition that handles natural speech, technical vocabulary, and multi-speaker dialogue. Then we translate with context-aware models that preserve intent and domain meaning — not just literal words.
how.p3: Next, Curify aligns translated subtitles to the original speech segments, optimizing timing and readability. We keep line length balanced, choose clean sentence breaks, and maintain a natural reading speed for stacked dual-language subtitles.
how.p4: Finally, Curify refines translations to better preserve tone — persuasive, educational, humorous, or dramatic — and ensures punctuation and rhythm feel natural in the target language.
how.p5: You can export subtitle files (e.g., SRT), download bilingual subtitle tracks, or embed bilingual subtitles directly into your video — and edit timing, translation, and styling before publishing.

usecases.title: Who Uses Bilingual Subtitles?
usecases.creatorsBody: Creators use bilingual subtitles to reach international audiences without managing separate channels. Bilingual subtitles can increase watch time, improve engagement from multilingual viewers, and help content rank in international search. On short-form platforms where videos autoplay without sound, bilingual subtitles also boost retention and accessibility.
usecases.educationBody: Educational content benefits significantly from bilingual subtitles. Students can compare terminology across languages, retain vocabulary more easily, and follow complex explanations with less friction. This is especially useful for STEM lectures, certification courses, and language learning materials where accuracy and consistency matter.
usecases.businessBody: Businesses use bilingual subtitles for product demos, investor presentations, training videos, and marketing campaigns. Instead of producing separate localized videos, you can ship a single multilingual asset — reducing production cost while expanding global reach.

metadata.title: AI Video Dubbing Tool | Curify AI
metadata.description: Dub your videos into 170+ languages using AI voice cloning, emotion preservation, and lip sync. Try Curify's AI dubbing for free.

videoDubbing.title: AI Video Dubbing with Natural Voice
videoDubbing.description: Translate and dub your video content into over 170 languages with realistic AI voice and lip sync. Perfect for international creators and brands.
videoDubbing.example: Example: AI-dubbed video with emotion preservation and lip sync across languages.

demo.caption: Example: AI-dubbed video with emotion preservation and lip sync across languages
demo.aria: AI video dubbing demo across multiple languages

why.title: Why Choose Curify AI Video Dubbing?
why.point1: Natural AI voice cloning that preserves speaker emotion and tone
why.point2: Automatic lip sync for a seamless viewing experience
why.point5: Ideal for YouTube, TikTok, corporate training, and e-learning

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How accurate is the AI dubbing?
faq.a1: Curify uses advanced voice cloning and translation models designed to preserve the original speaker’s tone, intent, and pacing. Lip sync further improves perceived realism by matching speech timing to the speaker’s mouth movement.
faq.a2: Curify supports 170+ languages, including English, Spanish, Chinese, French, German, Japanese, Korean, Arabic, and many more.
faq.a3: Processing time depends on video length and complexity, but most videos are ready within minutes. You’ll see progress updates and get notified when the dubbed video is complete.

what.title: What Is AI Video Dubbing?
what.p1: AI video dubbing is the process of translating spoken dialogue into another language and generating a natural-sounding voice track that matches the original delivery. Unlike subtitles, dubbing replaces the audio — making your content feel native to viewers in each target language.
what.p2: High-quality dubbing requires more than translation. It must preserve meaning, tone, pacing, and emotion, while staying aligned with the speaker’s timing. For training content, product demos, and creator videos, this alignment is what separates “robotic” dubbing from professional localization.
what.p3: Curify is built for modern global content: one upload, multilingual output — optimized for creators, educators, and businesses that need speed and quality at scale.

how.title: How Curify Produces Natural Dubbing (Voice, Timing, and Lip Sync)
how.p1: Curify’s dubbing quality comes from a multi-stage pipeline designed for faithful meaning, natural prosody, and tight alignment to the original video.
how.p2: Step 1: High-accuracy transcription captures the original speech structure, including pauses, emphasis, and speaker turns. This becomes the timing backbone for the dubbing track.
how.p3: Step 2: Context-aware translation preserves intent and domain meaning — especially for technical terms, brand language, and instructional content. We avoid literal phrasing when it sounds unnatural in the target language.
how.p4: Step 3: Voice synthesis generates a natural voice track with controlled pacing. We optimize segment length and reading speed so the translated speech fits the original timing without sounding rushed or overly slow.
how.p5: Step 4: Optional lip sync aligns speech timing to mouth motion for a more immersive viewing experience — particularly important for face-to-camera videos, interviews, and training sessions.

usecases.title: Who Uses AI Dubbing?
usecases.creatorsTitle: For YouTube, TikTok, and Global Creators
usecases.creatorsBody: Creators use dubbing to reach new audiences without running separate productions. Dubbing makes content easier to consume than subtitles in many contexts, especially for entertainment, tutorials, and fast-paced short-form videos.
usecases.educationBody: Training teams and course creators use dubbing to localize onboarding, compliance, and product education. A natural voice track reduces cognitive load compared to reading subtitles — improving completion rates and learner retention.
usecases.businessTitle: For Product Marketing and Customer Education
usecases.businessBody: Global teams localize demos, explainers, webinars, and support videos with dubbing. Instead of recreating content per region, you can ship one video and deploy multiple language versions with consistent messaging.

compare.title: AI Dubbing vs Subtitles: Which Should You Use?
compare.p1: Subtitles are ideal when viewers are comfortable reading, when you want to preserve the original audio, or when you need the fastest publishing workflow. Dubbing is ideal when you want a native viewing experience, higher engagement, and less reliance on reading.
compare.p2: Many teams use both: subtitles for speed and accessibility, dubbing for top-performing content and key markets. Curify supports both workflows so you can choose the right format for each video.

metadata.title: AI Video Transcript Generator | Curify AI
metadata.description: Generate accurate transcripts from any video in minutes. Curify's AI transcript tool supports 170+ languages and exports to SRT and TXT.

videoTranscriptGenerator.title: AI Video Transcript Generator
videoTranscriptGenerator.description: Upload a video or paste a YouTube link and get a full, accurate transcript in seconds. Export as SRT or plain text — ready for captions, repurposing, or search indexing.
videoTranscriptGenerator.example: Example: A 10-minute lecture transcribed with speaker-accurate text and timestamps.

why.title: Why Use Curify for Video Transcription?
why.point1: High-accuracy transcription powered by advanced speech recognition models.
why.point2: Supports 170+ languages with automatic language detection.
why.point3: Export full transcripts as SRT (with timestamps) or plain TXT.
why.point4: Works with uploaded videos and YouTube links — no extra software needed.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What video formats does the transcript generator support?
faq.a1: Curify supports all common video formats including MP4, MOV, and AVI, as well as direct YouTube URLs. Just upload or paste and transcription begins automatically.
faq.q2: Can I edit the transcript after it's generated?
faq.a2: Yes. After generation you can review and download the transcript. SRT files include timestamps for use in video editors or caption tools.

what.title: What Is an AI Video Transcript?
what.p1: A video transcript is a text version of everything spoken in a video — including speaker dialogue, narration, and key audio cues. Transcripts are the foundation for captions, searchable content, repurposed articles, and accessibility compliance.
what.p2: AI transcript generators automate this process using speech recognition models trained on millions of hours of audio across dozens of languages. The output is a time-aligned text document — far faster and more scalable than manual transcription.
what.p3: Curify's transcript tool is designed for speed and accuracy: upload once, get a clean, editable transcript ready to export or use downstream in your content workflow.

how.title: How Curify Generates Accurate Transcripts
how.p1: Curify's transcript pipeline is built on production-grade speech recognition with post-processing tuned for real-world video audio — including background noise, multiple speakers, and accented speech.
how.p2: Step 1: Audio is extracted from your video and preprocessed to normalize volume levels and reduce background interference.
how.p3: Step 2: Our speech recognition model segments audio into speaker turns and converts each segment to text with high accuracy.
how.p4: Step 3: Post-processing adds punctuation, capitalizes proper nouns, and aligns each sentence to its timestamp in the original video.
how.p5: Step 4: You receive a clean transcript file — SRT for timestamped captions or TXT for plain text use in documents, articles, or search indexing.

usecases.title: Who Uses Video Transcription?
usecases.creatorsBody: Creators use transcripts to repurpose video content into blog posts, social captions, newsletters, and scripts. A single long-form video can fuel a week of content once transcribed.
usecases.educationBody: Transcripts make lectures, webinars, and training sessions accessible and searchable. Students can review material in text form and find key moments without rewatching the full video.
usecases.businessBody: Legal, compliance, and media teams use transcripts to archive meeting recordings, interview footage, and customer calls. Searchable transcripts save hours of manual review time.

metadata.title: AI Video Summarizer | Curify AI
metadata.description: Get an instant AI-generated summary of any video. Curify extracts the key points and saves you hours of watching time.

videoSummarizer.title: AI Video Summarizer
videoSummarizer.description: Upload a video or paste a YouTube link and get a concise, structured summary of the key points — in seconds. Perfect for meetings, lectures, interviews, and long-form content.
videoSummarizer.example: Example: A 45-minute podcast episode summarized into 5 key takeaways.

why.title: Why Use Curify to Summarize Videos?
why.point1: AI-generated summaries that capture the key points, not just a transcript.
why.point2: Works with uploaded videos and YouTube links.
why.point3: Export the summary as a plain text document ready to share.
why.point4: Supports 170+ languages with automatic source language detection.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Most videos are summarized within a few minutes depending on length. You'll see a progress indicator while Curify processes your video.
faq.a2: Curify summarizes based on the actual spoken content, not metadata or descriptions. Accuracy depends on audio clarity. You can download the summary as a TXT file and edit it as needed.

what.title: What Is an AI Video Summary?
what.p1: An AI video summary condenses the key ideas, arguments, or takeaways from a video into a short, readable format — without requiring you to watch the full content. It is generated from the spoken audio, not from video titles or descriptions.
what.p2: Unlike a transcript (which is a word-for-word record), a summary identifies the most important points, filters out filler, and presents them in a structured, scannable format. This is especially useful for long recordings where key moments are spread across hours of content.
what.p3: Curify generates summaries directly from speech, making them accurate to the actual content rather than relying on metadata that may not reflect what was said.

how.title: How Curify Summarizes Video Content
how.p1: Curify's summarization pipeline first transcribes the full spoken audio, then applies language models to extract and structure the most important content.
how.p2: Step 1: Full transcription captures everything spoken in the video, including speaker turns and segment timing.
how.p3: Step 2: The transcript is passed through a summarization model that identifies key themes, arguments, and conclusions.
how.p4: Step 3: The output is structured into a readable summary — with key points, section headings where relevant, and a concise conclusion.
how.p5: Step 4: The summary is exported as a plain text file ready for distribution, note-taking, or downstream content production.

usecases.title: Who Uses AI Video Summarization?
usecases.creatorsBody: Researchers use summaries to quickly assess whether a lecture, interview, or conference talk contains relevant material before committing to watching the full video.
usecases.educationBody: Meeting recordings, sales calls, and webinar replays are long. AI summaries let team members catch up on key decisions and action items without watching the full recording.
usecases.businessBody: Video summaries provide instant source material for newsletters, articles, show notes, and social posts — turning one long video into a ready-to-publish text asset.

metadata.title: AI Speech Translator — Translate Audio to Any Language | Curify AI
metadata.description: Translate spoken audio into 170+ languages with a natural AI voice. Curify's speech translator produces a dubbed video with the translated voice track.

speechTranslator.title: AI Speech Translator
speechTranslator.description: Upload a video or audio file and translate the spoken content into another language with a natural AI voice. Receive a fully dubbed video ready to publish — no recording studio needed.
speechTranslator.example: Example: A product demo recorded in English, translated and dubbed in Spanish and Japanese.

why.title: Why Use Curify for Speech Translation?
why.point1: Translates spoken audio into 170+ languages with natural AI voice synthesis.
why.point2: Produces a fully dubbed video — not just subtitles — with the translated voice track.
why.point3: Preserves speaker tone, pacing, and emotional delivery in the target language.
why.point4: Works with uploaded videos and YouTube links.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What is the difference between speech translation and video dubbing?
faq.a1: Speech translation focuses on translating the audio track of a video and replacing it with a natural AI voice in the target language. Video dubbing may additionally include lip sync alignment. Curify's speech translator produces a dubbed video with the translated voice track.
faq.a2: Curify supports 170+ languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Hindi, and many more. Language detection is automatic.

what.title: What Is AI Speech Translation?
what.p1: AI speech translation converts spoken words in a source language into natural spoken words in a target language — generating a new audio track in the translated language rather than displaying text subtitles.
what.p2: The output is a dubbed video: the original video with its audio replaced by a translated, AI-synthesized voice. This is the fastest way to make video content natively accessible to international audiences without requiring a recording studio.
what.p3: Curify's speech translator is designed for real-world video content — handling background noise, multiple speakers, technical vocabulary, and natural conversational pacing across 170+ languages.

how.title: How Curify Translates Speech
how.p1: Curify's speech translation pipeline combines high-accuracy transcription, context-aware translation, and natural voice synthesis in a single automated workflow.
how.p2: Step 1: Speech is transcribed from the source audio using production-grade speech recognition that handles accents, domain vocabulary, and multi-speaker dialogue.
how.p3: Step 2: The transcript is translated using context-aware models that preserve meaning, tone, and intent — not just literal word substitution.
how.p4: Step 3: A natural AI voice synthesizes the translated text in the target language, matching the original pacing and emotional delivery where possible.
how.p5: Step 4: The synthesized voice track replaces the original audio in the video, and you receive a fully dubbed video file ready to publish.

usecases.title: Who Uses AI Speech Translation?
usecases.creatorsBody: Creators use speech translation to reach audiences in new language markets without re-recording their content. One upload produces multilingual dubbed versions ready for different regional channels.
usecases.educationBody: Training departments translate onboarding videos, safety briefings, and product education into local languages. A natural voice track improves retention and engagement compared to subtitles alone.
usecases.businessBody: Marketing teams translate demo videos, customer testimonials, and event recordings into target market languages — enabling consistent messaging across regions without per-market production costs.

metadata.title: AI Video Enhance | Restore Archival & Low-Res Video | Curify AI
metadata.description: See an AI video enhancement demo on real archival footage. Curify restores detail, sharpness, and clarity in low-resolution and compressed video — sign up for early access.

videoEnhance.title: AI Video Enhancement
videoEnhance.description: Restore detail, sharpness, and clarity in low-resolution, compressed, or archival video. Toggle between the original and enhanced clips below to see the AI restoration on real footage.
videoEnhance.example: Example: a 1970s news segment on the oil crisis — original SD broadcast quality vs the AI-enhanced version.

demo.caption: Use the toggle to switch between the original footage and the AI-enhanced version.

why.title: Why Use Curify for Video Enhancement?
why.point1: Restores detail in archival, broadcast, and user-uploaded footage where source resolution is fixed.
why.point2: Removes compression artifacts and recovers texture without the over-sharpened, plastic look most upscalers produce.
why.point3: Works on full-length clips — not just short bursts — and keeps motion natural across cuts and pans.
why.point4: Demo-only today; sign up for early access to run your own footage when the pipeline opens.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Not yet — this page is a working demo on archival footage. The production pipeline is in private testing. Sign up for early access and we will reach out when it opens for upload.
faq.q2: What kinds of video does enhancement work best on?
faq.a2: It performs best on real-world footage that has lost detail to compression, low source resolution, or generational copy loss — archival broadcasts, old home video, security camera footage, and SD content uploaded as HD. It is not a face-restoration or generative re-creation tool — it restores detail that existed in the source signal.

what.title: What Is AI Video Enhancement?
what.p1: AI video enhancement reconstructs visual detail that was lost or degraded during recording, compression, or transmission. Unlike a simple resolution upscale, the goal is not larger pixels — it is recovering the texture, edges, and tonal range that a higher-quality capture of the same scene would have produced.
what.p2: Where traditional upscalers interpolate between existing pixels (producing a softer or sharpened-but-plastic image), modern AI restoration models learn from large datasets of paired low-quality and high-quality footage. They infer plausible high-resolution detail based on what the underlying scene most likely contained — preserving motion, lighting, and texture coherently across frames.
what.p3: The demo on this page enhances a clip from a 1970s news segment. The original is SD broadcast quality with the typical compression and chroma loss of the era. The enhanced version recovers facial detail, scene texture, and edge sharpness while keeping the era-appropriate film grain and color palette intact.

how.title: How Curify Approaches Video Enhancement
how.p1: Production-grade video enhancement is not a single model — it is a pipeline of specialized models that each handle one failure mode, sequenced so their corrections compound rather than fight each other.
how.p2: Step 1: Scene segmentation and shot detection. The pipeline splits the clip at scene boundaries so per-shot restoration parameters can adapt to lighting, motion, and content rather than averaging across the whole clip.
how.p3: Step 2: Per-frame restoration. Detail reconstruction, deblocking, and denoising are applied with awareness of motion vectors so corrections stay temporally consistent — frames do not flicker between detail levels across cuts.
how.p4: Step 3: Temporal stabilization. A second pass smooths residual flicker, aligns enhanced detail across frames, and preserves natural motion blur where intentional.
how.p5: Step 4: Quality-aware encoding. The final clip is encoded with a bitrate and codec choice that preserves the recovered detail rather than re-introducing the compression artifacts the restoration just removed.

usecases.title: Who Uses AI Video Enhancement?
usecases.creatorsBody: Producers working with archival footage, old interviews, or vintage broadcasts use enhancement to bring source material up to a quality that holds up on modern displays without re-creating the era-appropriate look.
usecases.educationBody: Libraries, museums, and educational publishers digitize tape and film collections at the original recorded quality. Enhancement recovers detail that lets the same footage be projected, embedded in courses, or licensed without the apologetic 'sorry for the quality' framing.
usecases.businessBody: Marketing teams revisit older brand assets — founder interviews, early product demos, broadcast spots — and want to repurpose them in modern campaigns. Enhancement closes the quality gap without re-shooting, preserving the original story and characters.

metadata.title: AI Style Transfer for Video | Pixar, Ghibli, Anime | Curify AI
metadata.description: Apply Pixar, Ghibli, anime, or any reference art style to a real-world video clip. Curify's style transfer pipeline preserves motion and identity while swapping the visual language.

styleTransfer.title: AI Style Transfer for Video
styleTransfer.description: Apply Pixar, Ghibli, anime, or any reference art style to a real-world video — frame-by-frame consistency, preserved motion, recognizable subjects. Watch the demo below for an example.
styleTransfer.example: Example: a live-action clip restyled into a Ghibli painterly look, frame-coherent across the full shot.

why.title: Why Use Curify for Style Transfer?
why.point1: Frame-coherent restyling — no flicker or identity drift between frames, even across cuts and pans.
why.point2: Style is set by a reference image or a named preset (Pixar, Ghibli, anime, watercolor, oil painting, etc.).
why.point3: Subjects stay recognizable — faces, props, and on-screen text survive the restyle.
why.point4: Demo-only today; sign up for early access to run your own footage when the pipeline opens.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Not yet — this page shows a working demo on a sample clip. The production pipeline is in private testing. Sign up for early access and we will reach out when it opens for upload.
faq.q2: What kinds of source video work best?
faq.a2: Footage with steady framing, clear subjects, and a defined foreground works best. Style transfer is a creative restyle, not a face-swap — the subjects stay recognizable but the visual language changes. Highly compressed or low-resolution footage will produce noisier output.

what.title: What Is AI Style Transfer for Video?
what.p1: Style transfer takes a source video and a target visual style — Pixar 3D, Ghibli painterly, anime line art, watercolor — and re-renders the clip in the target style while preserving the original motion, framing, and subject identity. The output is a new video, not a filter applied to the original frames.
what.p2: Image style transfer has been around for a decade, but the hard part for video is temporal coherence. A naive frame-by-frame restyle flickers — each frame is restyled independently, so colors and textures jitter as the model makes different choices on adjacent frames. Modern pipelines fix this by conditioning on the previous frame and propagating style decisions across the temporal axis.
what.p3: The demo on this page shows a live-action clip restyled into a Ghibli painterly look. The motion stays smooth, faces stay recognizable, and the color palette stays consistent across the full shot.

how.title: How Curify Approaches Style Transfer
how.p1: Production-grade video style transfer is a pipeline of specialized stages, not a single model call. Each stage handles one part of the temporal-coherence problem so the corrections compound rather than fight each other.
how.p2: Step 1: Shot detection and scene segmentation. The clip is split at shot boundaries so each shot gets its own style parameters and the model is not asked to maintain coherence across an unrelated cut.
how.p3: Step 2: Reference encoding. Either a style reference image or a named preset is encoded into a target style embedding that drives the restyle.
how.p4: Step 3: Frame-coherent restyling. Frames are restyled with awareness of the previous output frame, so colors, textures, and line weights stay consistent across motion.
how.p5: Step 4: Temporal smoothing and quality-aware encoding. A residual flicker pass cleans any remaining artifacts and the final clip is encoded at a bitrate that preserves the restyled detail.

usecases.title: Who Uses AI Style Transfer?
usecases.creatorsBody: Indie animators use style transfer to lock a consistent visual style across hours of footage without hand-painting every frame. The output is the starting point for downstream cleanup, not the final delivery — but it collapses days of frame-by-frame work into minutes.
usecases.educationBody: Educational creators translate live-action explainer videos into a friendlier painterly or 3D style for younger audiences. The same recording can ship in multiple visual registers for different age brackets without re-shooting.
usecases.businessBody: Marketing teams restyle a single live-action shoot into multiple stylistic variants — a clean corporate cut, a playful animated cut, a regional aesthetic cut — for split testing or multi-channel publishing without per-variant production cost.

metadata.title: AI Manga Translation | Translate Comic Panels with Layout Preserved | Curify AI

mangaTranslation.title: AI Manga Translation
mangaTranslation.description: Translate the text in manga, comics, and webtoons into 30+ languages while keeping the original bubble shapes, fonts, and panel layout intact. Watch the before / after demo below.
mangaTranslation.example: Example: a Chinese-language manga page translated into English and Spanish with the bubble layout preserved.

why.title: Why Use Curify for Manga Translation?
why.point1: Detects every speech bubble, narration box, and SFX label — not just dialogue lines.
why.point2: Rebuilds the typography to fit the bubble — line breaks, font weight, and vertical / horizontal orientation all adapt.
why.point3: Preserves panel layout, page direction, and SFX placement so the translated page reads natively in the target market.
why.point4: Demo-only today; sign up for early access to translate your own pages when the pipeline opens.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Not yet — this page is a working demo on sample pages. The production pipeline is in private testing. Sign up for early access and we will reach out when it opens for upload.
faq.a2: The translation layer supports 30+ languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Korean, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and Arabic. Language detection is automatic on the source side; the target language is the user choice.

what.title: What Is AI Manga Translation?
what.p1: Manga translation is a multi-stage task: detect the text on the page, recognize what it says, translate it into the target language, fit the translation back into the original bubble or panel space without breaking the typesetting. Most generic translation tools handle only the middle step and produce unusable pages that need full re-layout.
what.p2: The hard part is not translation quality — it is layout preservation. A speech bubble shaped for vertical Japanese text needs to hold a much longer English sentence without overflowing, losing reading order, or covering the artwork. SFX (sound effects) drawn into the panel art are even harder: they have to be removed cleanly and re-rendered in the target language without damaging the underlying drawing.
what.p3: The demo on this page shows a sample page translated end-to-end with the bubble layout, font choices, and SFX placement all preserved in the output.

how.title: How Curify Approaches Manga Translation
how.p1: The pipeline runs four specialized stages, each handling one failure mode of the naïve translate-and-overlay approach.
how.p2: Step 1: Bubble and text region detection. A vision model identifies every speech bubble, narration box, SFX label, and free-floating text region, with bounding polygons that respect the original layout.
how.p3: Step 2: OCR and source recognition. The text inside each region is recognized — handling vertical scripts, hand-drawn lettering, and stylized SFX fonts that defeat general-purpose OCR.
how.p4: Step 3: Context-aware translation. The translation step preserves character voice across pages (so the same character speaks consistently) and adapts SFX to target-language conventions where appropriate.
how.p5: Step 4: Typographic re-layout. Translated text is re-rendered in the original bubble with adaptive line breaks, font weight matching, and orientation switching (vertical → horizontal where needed) so the output reads natively in the target market.

usecases.title: Who Uses AI Manga Translation?
usecases.creatorsTitle: For Indie Comic and Webtoon Creators
usecases.creatorsBody: Indie creators publish in one language and want to reach readers in others without paying per-page localization fees. Automated translation produces a draft that the creator (or a community translator) can polish, collapsing weeks of layout work into minutes.
usecases.educationTitle: For Language Learning and Bilingual Editions
usecases.educationBody: Language teachers and bilingual-edition publishers produce side-by-side or alternating-language pages from a single source. The translation pipeline outputs a layout-preserved version in each target language, ready for typesetting into the final bilingual edition.
usecases.businessTitle: For Manga, Comic, and Webtoon Publishers
usecases.businessBody: Publishers localize backlist titles into new markets where licensed manual translation is uneconomical. Automated translation makes a long-tail localization strategy viable — every backlist title becomes a candidate for a new-market release.
